C-CODEX-PRESET-LIMITS.Summary: Rename the managed OpenAI preset and aliases to explicit Codex subscription terminology and apply subscription-safe token limits.
C-CODEX-PRESET-LIMITS.Problem: The current vv-gpt aliases inherit direct-API input limits from models.dev while they are used through Codex subscription routing, so OpenCode delays compaction beyond the server-enforced input budget and long GPT-5.6 sessions fail near 372000 input tokens.

Goals.Goal: Make vv-codex the canonical managed preset name instead of vv-openai.
Rename managed OpenAI aliases to the vv-codex-gpt-* namespace while retaining provider.openai as the target provider.
Set explicit context, input, and output limits for every managed Codex alias so no direct-API input limit is inherited.
Make codex the canonical patch-provider preset while preserving the old openai command spelling as a compatibility alias.
Update osovv presets, defaults, documentation, completions, and tests to use the canonical Codex names.

NonGoals.NonGoal: Create a separate OpenCode provider ID for Codex subscriptions.
Optimize managed aliases for directly billed OpenAI API users.
Delete legacy vv-gpt-* aliases from user-owned OpenCode configuration automatically.
Fix OpenCode or CLIProxyAPI transport-level GPT-5.6 Responses Lite behavior.

AcceptanceCriteria.Criterion: Fresh managed vvoc configuration contains vv-codex and no canonical vv-openai preset entry.
vv-codex, vv-osovv, and vv-osovv-cheap reference openai/vv-codex-gpt-* models where managed Codex aliases are used.
The Codex patch writes vv-codex-gpt-5.4-mini-low with limits 400000 context, 272000 input, and 128000 output.
The Codex patch writes vv-codex-gpt-5.5-xhigh with limits 400000 context, 272000 input, and 128000 output.
The Codex patch writes vv-codex-gpt-5.6-terra-high and vv-codex-gpt-5.6-sol-xhigh with limits 500000 context, 372000 input, and 128000 output.
vvoc patch-provider codex is canonical and vvoc patch-provider openai resolves to the same conservative provider.openai patch for compatibility.
Existing unrelated provider.openai options, models, and user-owned configuration remain preserved.
README examples and command descriptions use vv-codex and the vv-codex-gpt-* namespace.
Targeted preset, completion, config, and patch-provider tests plus the repository check and build gates pass.

VerificationIntent.ExpectedCommand: bun test src/commands/patch-provider.test.ts src/commands/preset.test.ts src/commands/completion.test.ts src/commands/config-validate.test.ts src/commands/launch.test.ts
VerificationIntent.ExpectedEvidence: Fresh output proves canonical names, compatibility routing, explicit subscription-safe limits, conservative config merging, and valid GRACE state.

Assumptions.Assumption: Managed vv-codex aliases primarily serve ChatGPT Codex subscription users, including users routed through CLIProxyAPI.
Provider ID openai remains the interoperable namespace for built-in OpenCode OAuth, direct API authentication, and OpenAI-compatible proxy baseURL overrides.
Using lower Codex limits through a directly billed API remains functionally safe even though it compacts earlier than the API maximum.
The user explicitly approved this design and requested implementation without another approval round.
